title: The Treehouse Storm
story_html:
Diego was hardworking, and this summer he set himself one goal: complete a treehouse in the big oak tree behind his house before school started again.

He made a plan with his dad, sketching every board on paper before they began. Diego had a habit of waking up early to work on the site before the sun got too hot.

Diego's neighbor, Mr. Alvarez, was wealthy and had a shed full of tools. Diego asked if he could borrow a hammer and some rope, and Mr. Alvarez was happy to help.

Building was not always easy. Some boards felt weak, and tying strong knots was difficult at first. Diego had to learn how to pull each board up slowly and hang it in place without losing control.

One afternoon, while digging near the roots, Diego found evidence of an old rope swing — a small mystery from long before his family moved in. He decided to keep the rusty hook as a little secret, tucked inside a wooden box on a shelf.

As the days passed, Diego's excitement began to increase, and every evening he checked the sky, hoping the weather would stay calm.

Then dark clouds gathered fast, and thunder made a low sound in the distance. A storm was coming quickly. Diego and his dad worked fast to roll up the tarp covering the wood and secure it tight.

By the time the rain stopped, they were soaked, and Diego grabbed a towel to dry off. He still felt hopeful, and the very next weekend, the treehouse was finally finished. Diego climbed up and tied a small flag to the roof, proud of everything he and his dad had built together.

hardworking.def: Working with a lot of effort and energy.

borrow.def: To take and use something that belongs to someone else, and give it back later.

control.def: The power to direct or manage something.

habit.def: Something you do often, almost without thinking.

secret.def: Something known only to a few people, kept hidden.

increase.def: To become larger or greater in amount.

weak.def: Not having much strength or power.

help.def: To make something easier for someone else.

learn.def: To gain new knowledge or a new skill.

mystery.def: Something strange that is hard to explain.

hang.def: To attach something so it does not touch the ground.

hopeful.def: Feeling positive that something good will happen.

towel.def: A cloth used to dry yourself.

flag.def: A piece of cloth with a design that represents something.

plan.def: An idea for how to do something.

keep.def: To have and hold onto something.

roll.def: To turn over and over, or to wrap something into a tube shape.

storm.def: Very bad weather with wind and rain.

site.def: A place where something happens or is built.

questions.qtext: In "Diego was hardworking, and this summer he set himself one goal," what does hardworking mean?
options: working with a lot of effort and energy
questions.explain: B — Diego put a lot of effort into finishing his treehouse, which shows he was hardworking.

questions.qtext: In "Diego asked if he could borrow a hammer and some rope, and Mr. Alvarez was happy to help," what does borrow mean?
options: to take and use something, then give it back later
to buy something at a store
questions.explain: A — Diego wanted to use Mr. Alvarez's tools temporarily, which is what borrowing means.

questions.qtext: In "Diego found evidence of an old rope swing — a small mystery from long before his family moved in," what does evidence mean?
questions.explain: B — The rusty hook was a sign, or proof, that a rope swing had once been there.

questions.qtext: Why did Diego and his dad have to work quickly near the end of the story?
options: Diego's dad had to leave for work
A storm was coming and they needed to protect the tarp and wood
Mr. Alvarez asked for his tools back
questions.explain: C — Dark clouds gathered and a storm was coming, so they hurried to roll up the tarp and secure the wood.

questions.qtext: How did Diego feel after the storm passed and the treehouse was finished?
options: Disappointed that the treehouse was not bigger
Proud and hopeful after finishing what he had worked hard on
Angry that the rain had ruined his work
questions.explain: B — Diego stayed hopeful through the storm, and felt proud once he tied the flag to the finished treehouse.
